[2018-08-10 18:22] cactus_wren (cactus): i think if a game is too balanced it's not as fun, although it may be a "better game"

[2018-08-10 18:24] cactus_wren (cactus): if a game is perfectly balanced and 0 variance then the player that plays better will win every game, which means if a player is sufficiently better than their opponent they will win close to 100% of the time
[2018-08-10 18:24] cactus_wren (cactus): which is boring and frustrating for the worse player
[2018-08-10 18:24] cactus_wren (cactus): there's a reason people like mtg and hearthstone, you can get lucky and win even if you're trash
[2018-08-10 18:26] cactus_wren (cactus): and i think having player favored sets is actually good for prismata because it hits this beautiful middle ground where there's 0 variance once the game starts, but sometimes a worse player starts with a huge advantage
[2018-08-10 18:27] cactus_wren (cactus): i have no idea what the solution would be for tournament play but for ladder, embargos seem like a great solution

[2018-08-10 20:38] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): But those other games have hidden information and variance introduced throughout the game, in prismata the only randomness is the set being rolled.  That randomness sometimes being game-deciding feels out of place since it's deterministic after that point. Like if you had a chess tournament where you rolled 2d6 at the start of the game, then doubles means you start down a knight, 7 means you start up a knight.  Yes you could overcome the disadvantage, but how does that make the game any better?

[2018-08-20 06:48] kaelii: And if you're buying two of them to ampo you need to have bought about 6 conduits.
[2018-08-20 06:48] kaelii: For roughly a 4 tarsier ampo, which is probably less impressive than a 4 tarsier ampo.

[2018-08-20 06:51] kaelii: Which also has some slight order matters nit picks.
[2018-08-20 06:52] kaelii: But mostly I figure shift click parasites, then click 1 by 1 to assign single damage to conduits. Shift clicking conduits targets highest health conduits each I guess?
[2018-08-20 06:52] kaelii: If you could even do that.
[2018-08-20 06:54] namington: say you have a 3 health conduit, 1 health conduit, 1 of these things that youre clicking, and a 2 health unit that you want to keep alive

[2018-11-29 22:21] zera777 (Zera): @Elyot Have you read this website? It's quite nice.
<https://yujiri.xyz/prismata/changes.html>
This article reminded me that Steelforge needs a subtle nerf. I know Random units are supposed to be strong, but they shouldn't beat Base units 100% of the time. Currently, Steelforge is strictly better than Blastforge in all sets without Drake or Grenade Mech. I recommend adding a +1 tech or +2 energy to Steelforge's cost. Perhaps blue, as it's already a blue sink.

I also think Ebb Turbine should be nerfed so the drones sacrificed give 2EE instead of 3E. This will give it more synergy with energy units but make it less "buy this or die" in high econ games.
